paths

add structured logging and request tracing in `mcp_service.py` (tool name
latency
exception class)

threshold methods
ROI methods) using `tests/testdata` fixtures

batch

success_factors: Workflow completed end-to-end with status=success across all expected nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-first adapter strategy was appropriate for PlantCV (import feasibility 0.9, low intrusiveness risk).
Generated MCP service started healthy over stdio, indicating baseline runtime viability.
Repository structure is well-organized and test-rich, enabling reliable function discovery and endpoint extraction.
failure_reasons: Validation depth is limited: original project tests did not pass/run in this execution context despite reported 100% coverage metadata.
Endpoint registry quality issue: duplicate tool names ('main' appears three times) creates ambiguity and potential dispatch/runtime conflicts.
Observability/performance data is effectively missing (0 memory/cpu/latency/throughput), preventing evidence-based optimization.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; repository imported via zip fallback (598 files), which is acceptable but may be slower/less deterministic than git clone with pinned commit.
node_performance.analysis_time: Successful static and structural analysis with broad module discovery; DeepWiki failed but LLM direct analysis provided sufficient fallback coverage.
node_performance.generation_time: Generation succeeded and produced expected MCP artifact set (service, adapter, entrypoint, requirements, README).
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health check passed, but no substantive endpoint smoke/integration tests are evidenced; original project test execution outcome is inconsistent and should be treated as unverified.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown in practice; reported 0 MB indicates instrumentation gap rather than true usage.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown in practice; reported 0% indicates missing telemetry.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low-to-moderate footprint expected from generated files, but reported size=0 indicates artifact sizing telemetry is incomplete.
